summary.intent: Address two P2 spec-conformance findings from a Codex review on PR #669: (1) Escape key in inline completion modes should restore the typed prefix rather than close, and (2) InlineCompletion live-region announcements should include the highlighted suggestion label.
summary.outcome: Both spec regressions were fixed with new failing tests written first, implementation patched, and all tests passing. The fix was committed as 'fix(components): complete combobox inline completion review' and pushed, with Codex review threads resolved.

repo: The combobox component lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has a formal spec at spec/components/selection/combobox.md that governs behavior.
Codex bot reviews PRs via @codex mention and posts inline review threads; reactions (👀, 👍) signal review state — 👀 means in-progress, 👍 means approved.
The combobox previously had no state to track the typed prefix before inline completion replaced the input value, which caused Escape to either close or leave divergent state.
InlineCompletion live-region announcements were missing the highlighted suggestion label, violating the accessibility spec.

code.finding: Core state struct — inline_completion_prefix field was added here to remember the typed prefix before inline completion autocompletes the input.
Escape key handling and input_change_values logic — Escape now routes through a clear-completion path that restores the stored prefix instead of closing.
Live-region / announcement logic — InlineCompletion announcements now append the highlighted suggestion label to conform with the spec.

workflow: Test-first approach: failing regression tests were written and confirmed to fail before implementing the fix, ensuring the tests actually exercise the new behavior.
The CI/Codex polling loop used 120-second sleep intervals with gh API GraphQL queries to check for unresolved threads and reaction states.
cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo llvm-cov, and git diff --check were all run as a pre-commit verification gate.
Codex threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation after posting an inline reply referencing the fixing commit SHA.

friction: Codex review took an unusually long time (~10+ minutes of polling) before posting its findings, requiring many defensive poll iterations.
Multiple apply_patch iterations were needed to get the implementation right, suggesting the inline completion state machine is complex and required multiple refinement passes.
The implementation required reading non-contiguous sections of a large file (mod.rs) to understand the full state flow.
open_items: Further Codex review passes may surface additional spec-conformance issues beyond the two P2 findings addressed in this session.
Coverage of inline completion edge cases may be incomplete — only the two Codex-flagged paths were explicitly tested.
